evidence_quote: Business continuity plan (BCP): Documented procedures that guide the university to recover, resume, and restore to a pre-defined level of operation following disruption.
catalog_notes: University-wide BCM policy administered by the Office of Business and Finance BCM Office; requires units to maintain BCPs, conduct BIAs every two years, risk assessments every three years, and report RTOs/RPOs. References ISO 22301 in external resources.

evidence_quote: Business Continuity Plans contain a collection of resources, procedures, actions and information that have been developed, tested, and are held ready to use in the event of a disaster or major disruption of operations.
catalog_notes: Published Business Continuity Program (policy SAFE028) with Appendix A list of departments required to plan and Appendix B BCP template focused on essential functions, RTO ('Maximum Time to go without Essential Function'), communications, and supplier MOUs.

evidence_quote: The purpose of the Policy is to provide reasonable, but not absolute, assurance that DePaul's business will continue as soon as possible after any incident that disrupts some or all of the university's essential business operations.
catalog_notes: Business Continuity Management Policy defining a six-step unit process (BIA, risk assessment, risk reduction, BCP development with succession plans and alternate facilities, training, biennial exercise). Establishes RTOs and RPOs.

institution_name: University of Michigan (Human Research Protection Program)
evidence_quote: This Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is meant to guide the development of contingency plans to support operations in the Human Research Protection Program (HRPP) during an emergency, disaster, or significant disruption to university systems affecting institutional or research operations.
catalog_notes: Department-level COOP for U-M's HRPP/IRB operations. Notes all U-M units are expected to maintain plans covering essential functions, staffing, supplies, communications, and contingency planning. Tied to Regents SPG 201.27 emergency reduction in operations.

evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is a tool used to help each department or business unit continue to function as a viable part of the entire organization in the event of an emergency.
catalog_notes: Emergency-management COOP program page. All units required to complete and submit a COOP, identifying critical processes, analyzing business impacts, mitigating risks, and exercising the plan.

evidence_quote: A continuity planning template has been designed by the University for departments to use as a guide or initial starting point when beginning the process of developing their own continuity of operations plans.
catalog_notes: EHS-run annual COOP review program with a university-designed continuity planning template (linked Google Doc) for departments. Guidance covers critical operations, remote work, IT dependencies, relocation needs, and alternate vendors.

evidence_quote: A Business Continuity Plan (BCP) takes a snapshot of everything your department does on an annual basis, with a list of the resources it needs to function. The point of a BCP is to address abnormal situations and then get you back on track to operating as normal.
catalog_notes: University-wide BCP program coordinated by Emergency Management & Continuity; every department on every campus is required to have a BCP, built in the IU Ready (Kuali Ready) tool.

evidence_quote: It is impossible to plan for everything, so the university has taken an "all-hazards" approach to continuity planning — meaning we plan for restoring services no matter what the cause. The university and all colleges, departments, and units on campus are required to develop and maintain a business continuity plan.
catalog_notes: Division of Public Safety / Emergency Management continuity planning program. Units build plans in the web-based Illinois Ready (Kuali Ready) tool; living document with annual testing (tabletop/exercise/drill).

evidence_quote: UIC's approach to organizational resilience is grounded in a two-part planning process supported by the Riskonnect BC&R platform. This ensures our critical functions are protected and our campus is prepared to respond quickly and effectively to unexpected disruptions.
catalog_notes: Office of Preparedness and Response Business Continuity & Resilience program using the Riskonnect BC&R platform; two-part process of Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and Business Continuity Planning (BCP) across academic, research, healthcare, and administrative units.

framework_alignment: FEMA five-phase (Prevention, Mitigation, Preparedness, Response, Recovery)
evidence_quote: In close collaboration with campus departments, we ensure the seamless continuity of operations by developing their Continuity of Operations Plans (COOPs).
catalog_notes: UWPD Emergency Management Unit develops campus department COOPs alongside the campus EOP and building Occupant Emergency Plans (OEPs); plans tested via workshops, tabletop, functional, and full-scale exercises.

evidence_quote: Business Continuity Planning (BCP) provides a framework for departments to maintain or quickly resume critical functions in the event of a disruption. Disruptions may include technology failures, facility closures, natural disasters, or other emergencies.
catalog_notes: Office of Audit, Risk, and Compliance BCP program using the OHIO Ready (Kuali Build) tool; documents critical functions with criticality levels (Critical 1-3 or Deferrable), key personnel/backups, dependencies, vital records, and annual review with tabletop testing.

evidence_quote: Business continuity refers to Northwestern's ability to maintain operations during and after an emergency or crisis event. If business continuity is effectively in place, Northwestern's key academic, research, and administrative functions will be minimally impacted by a disruptive incident.
catalog_notes: Business continuity coordinated, facilitated, and overseen by the Risk Management Office; all administrative, academic, and research areas are responsible for developing a business continuity plan. Dedicated Business Continuity Planning Office.

evidence_quote: A Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) addresses these topics and generally covers a short to mid-term range (roughly 1-30 days). Long-term recovery efforts, such as rebuilding facilities, are addressed in separate recovery plans.
catalog_notes: University Continuity Program coordinated by Emergency Management, guided by a cross-divisional Continuity Steering Committee; spans academic, research, and administrative continuity plus a separately charged IT Disaster Recovery Plan led by an IT DR Advisory Board.

evidence_quote: Business continuity planning at Wright State is organized based on three planning phases: building a Site Emergency Management plan, conducting a business process analysis and building the business continuity plan.
catalog_notes: Risk Management business continuity program structured in three sequential phases; the BCP phase builds plans around three outage scenarios (loss of location, loss of essential systems, loss of essential personnel). Quote taken from official Wright State page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: The Business Continuity Plan is designed to focus on carrying on operations to support the mission of the university– scholarship, research and service – despite crises of varying magnitude.
catalog_notes: University-wide business continuity program under the Division of Public Safety's Office of Resiliency; departments nominate a continuity coordinator who works with the business continuity manager to compile plan components. Quote taken from official case.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: The EOP is a fundamental component of emergency management when used in conjunction with each department's Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP), Building Emergency Plans (BEP), and the Integrated University Safety Plan.
catalog_notes: CSU's emergency plans framework pairs the campus Emergency Operations Plan (published EOP Base Plan PDF) with each department's COOP, Building Emergency Plans, and an Integrated University Safety Plan, built per NIMS/FEMA. Quote taken from official csuohio.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: COOP is an effort within individual departments and agencies to ensure continuity of their essential functions across a wide range of emergencies and events.
catalog_notes: EMU Emergency Management Office COOP program; one of a network of plans (Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan, COOP, Building Emergency Plans, Multi-Hazard Mitigation Plan). Units complete COOPs from templates with EMO help. Quote taken from official emich.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

framework_alignment: FEMA / U.S. Department of Education higher-ed emergency management
evidence_quote: Marquette's Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is based on directives from the Federal Emergency Management Agency and the Department of Education's plan for Emergency Management in Higher Education institutions.
catalog_notes: University-wide COOP organized around Primary Mission Essential Functions (MEFs), prioritizing human safety/food/shelter then teaching/technology then support areas. Business Continuity Annex lists leadership, communication, resources, staff, vital records, technology, and equipment. Quote taken from official marquette.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: Each University Dean and Department Head will develop and implement an Emergency Action Plan and Business Continuity plan for each of their respective areas of responsibility.
catalog_notes: University-wide Continuity of Operations Plan (published PDF on official utoledo.edu host) covering maintenance of critical operations including public safety, clinical operations, and student support; deans/department heads develop EAPs and BCPs and designate essential personnel. Quote taken from official UToledo HR continuity-of-operations page via search snippet; PDF and page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: The Emergency Management staff handle the university's business continuity planning to ensure that the university can maintain critical operations and services in the face of a disruptive event.
catalog_notes: Business continuity run by Public Safety Emergency Management with a dedicated Director of EM/Business Continuity and a Continuity Planning & Grants Specialist; program uses risk assessments, business impact analyses, and scenario-based planning across academic and administrative functions. Quote taken from official uc.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: The UW-Stevens Point Campus Overall Continuity of Operations (COOP) Plan establishes guidance for the methods, procedures and protocols that the University uses to recover and restore performance of the campus' primary mission essential functions that may be disrupted by man-made or natural disasters.
catalog_notes: Published campus-wide full COOP organized around FCD-1 elements: mission essential functions, delegations of authority, orders of succession, contingency staffing, alternate facilities, interoperable communications, vital records, with a 12-hour recovery objective and training. Quote taken from official UWSP COOP PDF via search snippet; PDF returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations and Emergency Management Team is responsible for planning for a campus response to several types of crises/emergencies.
catalog_notes: Safety & Risk Management hosts a Continuity of Operations and Emergency Management Team responsible for planning campus response to crises/emergencies; published Emergency Response Guide covers procedures and campus closures. Quote taken from official uwstout.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

evidence_quote: The plan's critical goals are the preservation of life, the protection of property, and continuity of academic and business operations.
catalog_notes: Continuity of academic and business operations is a named critical goal of Purdue's Integrated Emergency Management Plan (IEMP), an all-hazards NIMS/ICS-based plan reviewed annually; the IEMP also promotes a means to recover mission-critical business and academic operations. Quote taken from official purdue.edu IEMP PDF via search snippet; the large PDF returned a fetch rate-limit/size error at time of access.
